ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Cheesy Yumburger; FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is the closest parent category covering beef-patty bun sandwiches.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKV (Hamburger) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Hamburger) is the closest parent concept; no specific concept exists for Cheesy Yumburger.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Cheesy Yumburger weighs approximately 115 g and constitutes a single fast-food burger serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Fast foods, cheeseburger, regular, single patty, plain (FDC ID 173961)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 173961, which represents a generic fast-food cheeseburger (regular, single patty, plain). SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger sold at Bugis Village, Singapore is nutritionally comparable to a standard single-patty fast-food cheeseburger; no direct approved-source entry exists for this branded product. Values are used as the closest defensible analogue.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate using the University of Sydney GI Database value for a standard fast-food hamburger on a white-flour bun (GI ~66, medium category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (Am J Clin Nutr 2021;114:1625-1632). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (66 × 29.2) / 100 ≈ 19.2, where available carbohydrate per 115 g serving = 25.4 × 1.15 = 29.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (513 mg per 100 g; approximately 590 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of the Cheesy Yumburger due to its high sodium content (~590 mg per burger), which represents approximately 26% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 66) and moderate glycemic load (~19 per serving) from refined-flour bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume the Cheesy Yumburger in moderation; its medium GI (66) and glycemic load of approximately 19 per serving can cause a meaningful postprandial blood glucose rise, and the refined-flour bun should be accounted for within the individual's daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (5.8 g per 100 g; ~6.7 g per serving) and trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g) from beef patty and processed cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of the Cheesy Yumburger because its saturated fat (~6.7 g per serving) and detectable trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g) contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation; occasional consumption within a heart-healthy dietary pattern is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Cheesy Yumburger is a signature fast-food hamburger from Jollibee, a Filipino quick-service restaurant chain with outlets across Southeast Asia including Singapore (Jollibee Bugis Village). It consists of a seasoned beef patty topped with a processed American-style cheese slice, shredded lettuce, mayonnaise, and ketchup, served in a soft toasted white-flour bun. The patty is grilled and has a mildly sweet, savoury flavour profile characteristic of Jollibee's proprietary seasoning blend. The bun is notably softer and slightly sweeter than typical Western fast-food buns, reflecting Filipino taste preferences. Per 100 g, the burger provides approximately 272 kcal, 13.8 g protein, 13.5 g fat (of which 5.8 g is saturated), and 25.4 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 513 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), driven primarily by the refined-flour bun. Clinically, the Cheesy Yumburger is a moderate-energy, high-sodium, and moderate-fat fast food item that warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is certified Halal in Singapore. The 'D' suffix in the field survey listing likely denotes a deal or combo variant; nutritional values here reflect the standard single Cheesy Yumburger patty without additional sides or beverages.

confidence_reason: Nutritional values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173961) for a generic fast-food cheeseburger (regular, single patty, plain), which is an approved secondary global source. No direct approved-source entry exists for the Jollibee Cheesy Yumburger. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 for a comparable fast-food hamburger on a white-flour bun. Medium confidence is appropriate given the use of an adapted analogue rather than an exact match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC SR Legacy entry (FDC ID 173961) used as the primary source. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC SR Legacy entry used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC SR Legacy entry used. All -1 sentinel values are documented here per g3 requirements. The '(D)' suffix in the field survey listing (Cheesy Yumburger (D)) likely denotes a deal or combo variant on the delivery platform; the nutritional record here covers the standard single Cheesy Yumburger patty. Trans fat is present at approximately 0.3 g per 100 g from the beef patty (naturally occurring conjugated linoleic acid and vaccenic acid) and potentially from the processed cheese; this value is estimated from USDA FDC SR Legacy data for comparable products. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 173961, an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
